title: Winter Season Resources: Holiday Assistance & Finding Warm Spaces - 211info
text: Learn about additional resources available during the winter months, plus volunteering and donation opportunities for those looking to help others this season. The winter season means additional resources become available for the community, and it’s a period of high call volume at 211info, when many people look for additional help, and also ways to give back to their communities. Top requested winter season resources are: Holiday assistance programs offering gifts, meals, and free events Warm spaces like winter shelters and warming centers Information on where to donate or volunteer locally “Our staff often say that the holiday season is one of their favorite times to provide referrals, and that’s because there is often something unique to offer, or a person on the line looking to help their fellow neighbor,” said Cara Kangas, Director of Partnerships . “There is something special about helping someone find a program that brings joy, or connecting with a caller who wants to give back. Those moments remind us of the reason for the season.” Holiday Assistance Programs Holidays bring significant changes in available resources, and the availability of these programs changes quickly. The 211info database is updated daily to reflect the pace of new and closing holiday programs. Help during the holidays is also one of our most requested needs, and in 2024 we received 4,798 requests for holiday-related assistance . The top five needs were: Holiday gifts and toys Thanksgiving baskets Christmas baskets Thanksgiving meals Christmas meals It’s also important to remember that if you call but don’t find the help you need, check back often for new programs and services throughout the season. Also last year, 557 requests for holiday help went unfulfilled, either because programs were full or no services were available in the caller’s area. That’s why we rely on the community to help us identify local programs so we can share them with others to help as many people as possible. Do you have services to share with those in need this season? Or do you know of holiday services in your community? Help us bridge the gap by informing us of any local resources not currently listed in our database by emailing [email protected] , or filling out this form. Finding Warm Spaces The winter sheltering season runs from November 1 through March 31.During these months, there are several types of warming spaces available across Oregon and Southwest Washington. What you’ll find this season in our database: As our resource team continues updating winter and severe weather shelter sites and warming centers throughout Oregon and Southwest Washington, it can be helpful to know the different types of shelters you can find in our database during the colder months. Year-round shelters: Open year-round, with some opening extra space in winter Winter shelters: Open only throughout the entire winter season Severe weather shelters: Open by county officials when extreme weather conditions are expected Emergency warming centers: Daytime or evening access to heated facilities during extreme cold Between November 2024 and February 2025, our county and state partners asked 211info to expand our severe weather response for 13 days. During those expanded days, our team worked at increased capacity to monitor shelter space, coordinate transportation, provide daily reporting, and add extra staff to quickly answer severe weather calls. On top of our daily resource calls, we also answered an additional 1,600 severe weather calls during that period, and handled 446 requests for severe weather transportation to help people get to warm spaces and out of the cold. The availability of winter and severe weather warming spaces changes quickly depending on weather and available local resources (including volunteers, more on this up next). We encourage people looking for cold weather assistance to frequently check our website for updates, or give us a call to discuss alternative options if you’re not finding what you need. Make a Difference: Donations and Volunteering The winter season brings many ways to give back to your community. You can help by serving meals, donating gifts or warm gear, or contributing financially to local programs. Call 211 to see what programs are available in your area, and know that ongoing requests for winter shelter donations and warm gear are deeply needed and appreciated. To learn more about the types of donations needed this time of year, or other ways to get involved, visit our donation page . Mental Health During The Holidays The holidays can bring both joy and stress. Many people experience loneliness, grief, or isolation this time of year. Whether you’re seeking holiday assistance, a food pantry, or need assistance with bills, 211info is here to help. It’s also important to remember that 211info does not close for holidays, so we’re here Thanksgiving Day and the day after, Christmas Eve and Christmas Day, New Year’s Eve and New Year’s Day. Learn more about our core operating hours and when we are open for extended hours . We can also connect people to warmlines or talklines, and have access to hotlines for veterans, youth, older adults, and survivors of abuse. However, if you’re in crisis, please dial 988, and if you need emergency assistance please dial 911. For more information, check out our mental health resources . Originally published December 4, 2024. Updated October 7, 2025. Search Popular Posts Categories Equity News
